title: Aspartame and cancer - new evidence for causation.
ehr_evidence: None; the study uses laboratory animal data from the Ramazzini Institute.
summary: A re-evaluation of toxicological studies by the Ramazzini Institute confirming that aspartame causes dose-related increases in malignant tumors in rodents, including increased risk in offspring following prenatal exposure.
key_findings: Prenatal exposure to aspartame increases cancer risk in rodent offspring
